message:
test(golden): scrub latency_ms + add cass health --json golden assertion

Freeze the contract surface of `cass health --json` against a golden
so changes to the readiness payload shape require an explicit golden
regeneration.

- Extend `scrub_robot_json` with a fifth scrubber: `"latency_ms": <int>`
  → `"latency_ms": "[LATENCY_MS]"`. Keeping the field in the golden
  proves the shape is still emitted without locking in a value that
  varies run-to-run and by host. Quoting is intentional: the scrubbed
  value is a stand-in string, not an int, so downstream consumers
  reading the golden won't accidentally treat it as numeric.
- Add `health_json_matches_golden`: runs `cass health --json` inside
  an isolated TempDir HOME (via the existing `capture_robot_json`
  helper) and asserts the scrubbed output against
  `tests/golden/robot/health.json.golden`. The test comment documents
  what the readiness payload is expected to cover
  (status/healthy/initialized/errors/recommended_action plus per-
  subsystem state.* blocks) so the golden's scope is self-describing.

The golden file itself is regenerated on first run via
`UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 cargo test --test golden_robot_json -- health_json`
once the readiness payload lands in the next slice of bead ibuuh.9;
this commit reserves the test slot.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
